metadata.notes: Generated by Protocol Intelligence Platform. SR- IDs are placeholders.

general_information.notes: To be completed by Risk Manager upon IRMS entry

study_overview.title: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH PF-07220060 IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 YEARS AND OLDER WITH ER+/HER2- ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER

critical_factors.critical_data: DLT occurrence data (Phase 1b); Confirmed ORR per RECIST v1.1 (Phase 2); DoR, CBR, PFS, OS data; PK parameters (vepdegestrant, ARV-473, PF-07220060); Biomarker data (UGT2B7 variants, tumor genomics); ePRO data (EORTC QLQ-C30, EQ-5D-5L)
critical_factors.critical_process: Accurate tumor assessments and RECIST v1.1 evaluations by investigator; DLT observation period monitoring; Timely completion and submission of PRO questionnaires

critical_factors.critical_data: AE/SAE data; DLTs during Cycle 1; ECG abnormalities (QTcF prolongation); Clinical laboratory abnormalities (hepatic and renal function)
critical_factors.critical_process: Timely AE/SAE reporting and DLT evaluation; Routine monitoring of ECGs and lab values for dose modifications; Verification of resolution of prior toxicities to Grade ≤1

critical_factors.factor_name: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
critical_factors.critical_data: Concomitant medication data (especially CYP3A/UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers and PPIs); PK sample collection dates and times relative to dosing; Tumor assessment scans
critical_factors.critical_process: Strict adherence to prohibited medication washout periods (7-14 days prior to enrollment); Proper handling, processing, and shipment of PK and biomarker samples; Consistent scan modalities for tumor assessments

critical_factors.critical_data: ER+/HER2- status confirmation; Prior lines of therapy data; Baseline ECG (QTcF) and laboratory values (hepatic/renal); Contraception compliance data
critical_factors.critical_process: Verification of prior CDK4/6 inhibitor regimen for Phase 2; Confirmation of measurable disease per RECIST v1.1; Screening for prohibited medical conditions (visceral crisis, symptomatic CNS metastases)

critical_data.signal: ER+/HER2- A/MBC diagnosis and prior therapy requirements

critical_process.signal: Phase 2 specific requirement for prior CDK4/6 inhibitor

critical_factors.critical_data: Dosing data for vepdegestrant (QD) and PF-07220060 (BID); Dose reduction/modification data; Food intake timing relative to dosing
critical_factors.critical_process: Administration of both IPs with food; Synchronization of vepdegestrant and the first daily dose of PF-07220060 in the morning; Management of dose reductions (e.g., PF-07220060 level -1) due to unacceptable toxicity

critical_process.signal: Requirement to take with food and synchronize morning doses

risks.risk_description: Participant takes prohibited concomitant medication(s) (e.g., strong CYP3A/UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers, PPIs) or does not follow required washout periods.
risks.additional_context: The protocol strictly prohibits strong CYP3A and UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers, PPIs, and live vaccines due to significant drug-drug interaction risks with vepdegestrant and PF-07220060. PPIs must be switched to local antacids/H2-receptor antagonists 7 days prior to first dose.

associated_causes.cause: Participant uses over-the-counter medications, herbal supplements, or PPIs without informing the investigator.

usdm_trigger.signal: Prohibition of PPIs and herbal supplements

associated_causes.cause: Investigator oversight of complex DDI lists for CYP3A and UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers during the active treatment phase.

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Implement eCRF edit checks to flag the entry of prohibited concomitant medications (e.g., PPIs, strong CYP3A/UGT2B7 modifiers).
controls.additional_context: Focus specifically on the 7-14 day washout periods prior to enrollment.

controls.control_description: Routine clinical review of protocol deviations related to concomitant medications.
controls.additional_context: Ensure prompt review of any reported use of prohibited medications to assess impact on PK and safety.

intelligence.notes: The protocol strictly prohibits strong CYP3A and UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers, PPIs, and live vaccines due to significant drug-drug interaction risks with vepdegestrant and PF-07220060. PPIs must be swi

risks.risk_description: PK, biomarker, and tumor tissue samples incorrectly obtained, analyzed, stored, or handled.
risks.additional_context: The study requires extensive PK sampling for three entities (vepdegestrant, ARV-473, PF-07220060) and collection of tumor tissue/blood for exploratory biomarker analyses including UGT2B7 variants and genomic associations.

associated_causes.cause: Site staff unfamiliar with complex PK timepoints and processing requirements for multiple analytes.

usdm_trigger.signal: Plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060

associated_causes.cause: Improper collection, processing, or shipping of archival or fresh tumor blocks required for inclusion and biomarker analysis.

usdm_trigger.signal: Tumor block collected at time of diagnosis required

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

usdm_drivers.signal: Characterize plasma exposure and PK parameters
Explore tumor biology and potential treatment effects

controls.control_description: Provide protocol-specific training to site staff on the laboratory manual, focusing on PK draw timing relative to dosing and tumor tissue handling.
controls.additional_context: Emphasize the importance of accurate documentation of dosing times and PK draw times.

risks.risk_description: Enrolled participant(s) do not meet complex protocol eligibility criteria regarding prior therapies, organ function, or cardiovascular limits.
risks.additional_context: Eligibility is highly specific: Phase 2 requires exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor regimen and measurable disease. Strict cutoffs apply for QTcF (>470/450 msec), hepatic function (bilirubin, AST/ALT), and renal function (eGFR <50 mL/min).

associated_causes.cause: Misinterpretation of prior lines of therapy definitions, specifically distinguishing between Phase 1b and Phase 2 requirements.

usdm_trigger.signal: Phase 2 participants must have received 1 line of prior CDK4/6 inhibitor

associated_causes.cause: Inaccurate assessment of baseline ECGs or failure to correct electrolyte abnormalities prior to enrollment.

usdm_drivers.signal: Strict cardiovascular and organ function limits

controls.control_description: Medical monitor review of screening data (prior therapies, ECGs, labs) prior to enrollment approval.
controls.additional_context: Ensure specific verification of the CDK4/6 inhibitor requirement for Phase 2 participants.

intelligence.notes: Eligibility is highly specific: Phase 2 requires exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor regimen and measurable disease. Strict cutoffs apply for QTcF (>470/450 msec), hepatic function (bilirubin, AST/ALT),

risks.risk_description: Lack of appropriate or timely AE/SAE and DLT reporting during the critical Phase 1b dose-escalation period.
risks.additional_context: Phase 1b primary objective is safety/tolerability and DLT occurrence to determine the recommended Phase 2 dose. Timely reporting is critical for dose escalation decisions.

associated_causes.cause: Delayed reporting of clinical laboratory abnormalities or ECG changes by the site.

usdm_trigger.signal: Incidence of clinically significant laboratory and ECG abnormalities

associated_causes.cause: Misclassification or delayed communication of DLTs by investigators during the Cycle 1 observation period.

usdm_trigger.signal: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during Cycle 1

usdm_drivers.signal: Assess safety and tolerability to select recommended doses

controls.control_type: Monitors reinforce requirements to report late SAEs within 24 hours (per INV-GSOP-SD-GL)
controls.control_description: CRAs to reinforce strict 24-hour SAE reporting requirements and immediate notification of potential DLTs during site contacts.
controls.additional_context: Heightened monitoring focus during Phase 1b Cycle 1 for all enrolled participants.

intelligence.notes: Phase 1b primary objective is safety/tolerability and DLT occurrence to determine the recommended Phase 2 dose. Timely reporting is critical for dose escalation decisions.
